tools.name: list_sites
tools.description: List the sites this caller can analyze, in two groups. my_sites = the sites connected to the signed-in account (each with its display name + domain, so you can match phrases like "the production site" or "revenuescope.jp" without the user pasting a UUID); empty when the caller is not signed in. demo

tools.name: get_summary
tools.description: Return the full headline summary for a site and period in ONE call: the 5 KPIs (revenue, sessions, RPS, AOV, CVR) PLUS two engagement KPIs (avg_duration = average dwell time in seconds, bounce_rate = % single-page-exit sessions) each with value AND the period-over-period change vs the previous equal

tools.name: get_breakdown
tools.description: Consolidated breakdown tool. Pick `dimension`: 'channel' returns per-channel sessions/revenue/RPS plus engagement (visitors, avg dwell seconds, bounce rate) and bot_excluded_count (bot sessions removed from human metrics; a channel with sessions=0 but bot_excluded_count>0 is bot-only traffic, kept s

tools.name: get_keyword_performance
tools.description: Return search-query performance from Google Search Console for the given period. band='all' (default) returns per-query metrics — clicks/impressions/CTR/avg position/top landing page plus an estimated revenue per query (= 検索 organic RPS × clicks, a conservative estimate, 0 until the site has 検索 orga

tools.name: get_ai_traffic
tools.description: Return AI-assistant (ChatGPT/Claude/Perplexity/Gemini/Copilot) traffic for the given period. mode='referred' (default) lists landing pages that received clicked AI traffic — per page × AI source: sessions, bounce rate (%, always computed; judge reliability via the sessions count), summed revenue, an

tools.name: get_priority_insights
tools.description: Return the top 3 prioritized, pre-computed DIAGNOSES for the site over the given period — 'what should I act on this week', ranked by revenue impact. Unlike get_site_summary / get_kpi_summary / get_channel_breakdown (which return data), this applies a deterministic rule engine over KPI period-over-p

tools.name: suggest_budget_allocation
tools.description: Return a proposed monthly budget split across paid channels (meta/google/tiktok). site_id is OPTIONAL when the request is OAuth-authenticated. Path B (ad spend connected): precise weight = ROAS × (1 − saturation) with expected ROAS uplift. Path A (no ad spend): RPS-weighted proportional split with e

tools.name: get_page_trend
tools.description: Return how ONE page's Google Search performance changed over time (FD-040) — the time-axis drill-down for a page surfaced by get_breakdown(dimension='page'). Given a `page` (a normalized path like '/news/rps-revenue-per-session-guide' or a full URL — both resolve), returns a `series` of day or week

tools.name: get_content_actions
tools.description: Return a content 'playbook' for the site: every content page classified into ONE of five action buckets over a weekly-style window comparison (current window vs the immediately preceding window of equal length), ranked by search-opportunity × session gain so you can tell the user which page to GROW

tools.name: get_competitor_keywords
tools.description: Return the latest competitor SEO snapshot for the site (FD-041): which keywords each tracked competitor DOMAIN ranks for on Google (Japan/ja), at what position, with monthly search_volume, cpc and etv (estimated monthly traffic — a visit estimate, not a monetary value), plus how each rank moved vs t

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "get_page_trend" stacks urgency/override words (never, override). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Return how ONE page's Google Search performance changed over time (FD-040) — the time-axis drill-down for a page surfaced by get_breakdown(dimension='page'). Gi…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_content_actions" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Return a content 'playbook' for the site: every content page classified into ONE of five action buckets over a weekly-style window comparison (current window vs…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "get_content_actions" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "get_competitor_keywords" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "get_competitor_keywords" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Return the latest competitor SEO snapshot for the site (FD-041): which keywords each tracked competitor DOMAIN ranks for on Google (Japan/ja), at what position,…

title: Tool description changed since last scan
detail: Tool "get_content_actions" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash d440ebff)" → after: "Return a content 'playbook' for the site: every content page classified into ONE of five action buckets over a weekly-style window comparison (current window vs the immediately preceding window of equal length), ranked by search-opportunity × session gain so you can tell the user which page to GROW "

remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
